action_items.task: Stay within three feet of the kitchen outlet until the ankle monitor is fully charged (solid green light).
action_items.context: Monitor battery completely died and needs to recharge.

action_items.task: Move the ankle monitor base station permanently to the bedroom outlet by the closet.
action_items.context: Ensure the monitor is on a dedicated outlet to prevent tripped breakers.

action_items.task: Drop off the food sales permit application at City Hall downtown.
action_items.context: Applying for a food permit for additional income.

action_items.task: Provide a copy of the approved city food permit to the PO.
action_items.context: Required for the PO to add food sales to the client's approved employment file.

action_items.task: Wait for maintenance tech contact; if no call by Friday at noon, call BI Monitoring Center directly to schedule inspection.
action_items.context: Mandatory equipment inspection is required after a complete power failure.

action_items.task: Log the exact time of the monitor blackout and write up the incident as an equipment malfunction warning.
action_items.context: Official documentation of the dead monitor and tripped breaker.

critical_updates.details: Client's housemate/partner Hector moved out on Sunday. The household now consists solely of the client, her daughter, and her two grandsons.
Hector moved out of the home. Client is currently providing full-time childcare for her two grandsons while her daughter works double shifts at the hospital.
Client's ankle monitor completely died due to a tripped breaker. PO issued an official warning for equipment failure. Client denied any police contact.
Client is currently doing sewing and alterations full-time to compensate for lost income after Hector moved out. She is also in the process of applying for a city food sales permit.
Client denied any alcohol or substance use.
Client reported bad arthritis resulting from her sewing work, but stated she is managing it.

next_episode_context: Client had an ankle monitor power failure due to a tripped breaker and received a warning; she is currently tethered to a kitchen outlet while it recharges and must schedule a maintenance inspection by Friday. Her partner Hector moved out recently, causing a major household change. To replace lost income, she is sewing full-time and submitting a food sales permit application to the city on Thursday morning using an approved travel pass.
